description.value: Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.
Used for rolling to hit when fighting in ranged or melee combat, in place of any other skills. Does not use a modifier.
Used for rolling checks involving feats of physicality, including athletics and evasion. Does not use a modifier.
Used for rolling when making checks that require training or finesse. Note: if the task is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers accordingly. Does not use a modifier.
Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.
Injecting a target with Stim causes them to ignore all penalties from being Seriously Wounded for one hour. It can only be used on a target once per day.

Generic Excellent Qulaity Light Melee weapon.

+1 to Attack.
A very heavy pistol. It can't be concealed. If you roll 1 on Attack (Crit fail) the gun jams and requires using an action to clear.
A concealable SMG. If you roll 1 on Attack (Crit fail) the gun jams and requires using an action to clear.
Fragile Incendiary Grenade. Set a target Deadly on Fire (see CP:R p.180)

Hollow-point rounds designed to splinter on impact, embedding hot, painful shrapnel into the target. When you inflict a critical injury using this ammunition, the victim suffers the Foreign Object critical injury in addition to any other injuries rolled. Bonus damage is still only dealt once.

information.notes:
Mook Tier - Tier 2/Troublemaker -The low-level gangoons and enforcers that fill Night City's streets, trying to make a name for themselves. Not especially threatening, but they outnumber every other threat in the city 10:1.

Gang-Wide Ability - (Dis)Organized Crime: For every gangster in a scene, there’s a booster waiting somewhere nearby, just hoping for something to smash up. They enter the scene 1d3 rounds after hearing gunfire or being summoned.

Ability - Bloodthirsty: while wielding their light melee weapon, a single 6 is enough to cause the Foreign Object critical injury as the weapon is lodged into the target, disarming the Booster in the process.

Extra Gear - Choose between a poor-quality very heavy pistol & 16 expansive rounds and a poor-quality SMG & 60 basic rounds.

For every 3 Booster Gangsters in/entering a scene, 1 can be optionally equipped with a Molotov Cocktail. Lighting it (which enables its ability to be destroyed) requires an action and a free hand, prior to being thrown.

I remember back when I was a kid, when Boostergangs were still headline news. ‘Will your children be safe?’ Hah! Imagine what they would think of us now - Maelstrom psychoes running weapons across half the city, Reckoner suicide bombers on the daily and actual fucking kabuki ninjas running half the city. Ahh, the good old days…
